title: potamogeton × fluitans var. americanus
intro: Potamogeton × fluitans var. americanus, also known as American waterweed, is a hybrid aquatic plant species that belongs to the family Potamogetonaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and respiratory problems, due to its anthelmintic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of American waterweed include relief from coughs, colds, and other respiratory issues, as well as its potential to aid in digestion and reduce inflammation. American waterweed can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusate, and has been used in herbal medicine for centuries, particularly in traditional Indigenous American and European folk medicine.
